'''Skels''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that dwelled on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. Hunting in packs, they were a primitive people, attacking whatever they could find without warning. They could burrow under the snow, and survive in extremely cold conditions. The Skels resided in caves, within the mountains of Hoth. The species' name originated as a nickname given to them by combatants during the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]].

==Biology and appearance==
Skels were a bipedal [[Species/Legends|species]], and could grow up to 1.2 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height. They were naturally agile, and featured protective tusks that grew out of their snouts. The [[Limb/Legends|hands]] of the Skels boasted tough, [[Bone/Legends|bony]] claws which could be used to burrow under snow and ice. Their fur was dense and thick, insulating them against extremely cold-weather environments, such as [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. Such was the effectiveness of the fur that they could survive without shelter in conditions far colder than Hoth's average atmospheric temperature.<ref name="daa" />

==Society and culture==
[[File:Skels.jpg|thumb|left|175px|Skels would hunt in packs.]]
The Skels formed a primitive yet well-organized<ref name="SWTOR Codex Skel">{{TORcite|type=codex|Skel}}</ref> culture, and were aggressive by nature. They spent their time roaming the frozen wastelands of Hoth. Skels communicated through a [[Skel language|series of snarls and grunts]]. They lived in caves, located within Hoth's mountain regions, where they were provided with shelter. The Skels hunted in packs, and were brazen in their work, due to the scarcity of food on Hoth. As such, they would attack anything without provocation, including [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droids]]. Some Skels were able to fashion clubs from severed hands of other Skels.<ref name="daa" />

==History==
The Skels evolved on Hoth, a desolate, ice-covered planet<ref name="daa" /> located in the [[Anoat sector/Legends|Anoat sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Being the only sentients on the planet, the well-organized Skels considered it their property. During the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]], Hoth became the arena of a three-way conflict between the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]], the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] organization known as the [[White Maw]]. The Skels did not pick sides, regarding all belligerents as unwelcome invaders who should be driven off.<ref name="SWTOR Codex Skel" />

==Behind the scenes==
"The Skels of Hoth" were created by [[Brian Fraim]], a 22-year-old ''[[Star Wars]]'' fan, through the "[[Design An Alien]]" competition held by [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine|''Star Wars Galaxy'']] magazine. The competition, which was launched in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 3]] ([[1995]]), called upon readers to create entirely new alien species' influenced by what had been seen in the [[original trilogy]] of ''Star Wars'' films. Fraim's entry was not published alongside the winners in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]] ([[1996]]), and was instead displayed in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 10|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 10]] ([[1997]]) as an "honorable mention." Keeper of the [[Holocron continuity database]] [[Leland Chee]] later stated that successfully published "Design an Alien" materials were considered [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the 'Design an Alien' contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref> The Skels were not referenced in any other ''Star Wars'' works until 2011 with the release of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', however the word "skel" was used in [[Republic 65|''Republic'' 65]] ([[2004]]), as a derogatory term. It is unclear whether the expletive refers to this species.
